_source: OmniClass Table 13 — Spaces by Function (publicly-available mirrors per sprint-X-source-provenance.md §1)
_note: CRITICAL COVERAGE FINDING — OmniClass Table 13 (2012-05-16 edition, available via declared public secondary mirror at https://pdfcoffee.com/omniclass-13-2012-05-16-pdf-free.html) does NOT contain a residential parent category. The 16 confirmed parent categories present in the mirror are: 13-11 Space Planning Types, 13-13 Void Areas, 13-15 Wall Spaces, 13-17 Encroachment Spaces, 13-21 Parking Spaces, 13-23 Facility Service Spaces, 13-25 Circulation Spaces, 13-31 Education and Training Spaces, 13-33 Recreation Spaces, 13-35 Government Spaces, 13-37 Artistic Spaces, 13-41 Museum Spaces, 13-45 Library Spaces, 13-47 Spiritual Spaces, 13-49 Environmentally Controlled Spaces, 13-51 Healthcare Spaces. Confirmed verbatim from secondary mirror via WebFetch 2026-06-05 (commit-time): residential dwelling rooms (bedrooms, kitchens, dining rooms, living rooms, single-family / multi-family / dormitory / hotel / hostel / ancillary) are absent. Backup mirror (scribd.com/document/958191411) and primary NIBS wrapper PDF (NBIMS-US V3 §2.4.4.3) corroborate: no residential codes are extractable from publicly-available Table 13 sources. Per sprint-X-source-provenance.md §6 fabrication-prevention contract, residential entries are NOT synthesised from numbering patterns — the OmniClass code space for residential, if it exists, lives outside the 2012-05-16 edition's published Table 13 scope or under a separate licensed OmniClass table not consumed by Sprint X. Per Sprint X.B.1 NO FABRICATION constraint, this file ships with entries: [] (zero entries) and full gap disclosure. Future Sprint Y back-fill from canonical OCCS PDF (CSI licence) will determine whether OmniClass Table 13 publishes residential codes at all, or whether residential spaces are classified under a different OmniClass table (e.g. Table 21 Elements, Table 33 Disciplines). cross_references.cibse_lg + cross_references.nrm2 stay null per Sprint X deferral on all future entries. Coverage gap (target 80 vs actual 0): mirror provides 0% of expected OmniClass Table 13 residential entries; Sprint X.E.4 reviewer escalation expected per §6 SHIP-WITH-NOTED-CONCERNS path.

_mirror_consultation_log.verification_method: WebFetch 2026-06-05 — explicit search of all 16 parent categories for residential / dwelling / bedroom / kitchen / dining / living / bath / hotel / motel / hostel / dormitory / sleeping / guest room / apartment / condominium / housing / lodging / occupant rest / single-family / multi-family labels; zero matches
WebFetch 2026-06-05 — landing-page-only access; full PDF requires Scribd login (paid). Coverage corroboration deferred to Sprint Y.

WebFetch + local pdftotext 2026-06-05 — wrapper PDF contains scope statement, normative references, bibliography; no embedded code list. NIBS-V3 §2.4.4.3.4.1 incorporates OmniClass Table 13 by reference, not by re-typesetting. No residential codes extractable.

_escalation_required: TRUE — Sprint X.E.4 reviewer must decide: (a) accept residential.json as honest 0-coverage record under §6 SHIP-WITH-NOTED-CONCERNS verdict, OR (b) acquire CSI OCCS licence to determine whether OmniClass Table 13 residential codes exist outside the 2012-05-16 public-mirror scope, OR (c) re-scope room-types/residential.json to source from a different classification system (e.g. UniFormat, Uniclass 2015 Table SL Spaces/Locations, NRM3 elemental cost groups). Recommendation: route (a) for Sprint X close-out; route (b) for Sprint Y back-fill; defer route (c) until Sprint Z standards-scaling phase.
